maml:description.maml:para: Converts a project to a consistent encoding/line ending policy and reports the results.
This cmdlet applies a consistency policy (encoding and/or line endings) across a project tree. It can also export a post-conversion report so you can validate what remains inconsistent.
For build-time enforcement, use New-ConfigurationFileConsistency -AutoFix in the module build pipeline.
Backup root folder (mirrors the project structure).
Create backup files before modifying content.
Custom file extensions to include when ProjectType is Custom (e.g., *.ps1, *.cs).
Per-path encoding overrides (hashtable of pattern => encoding).
Ensure a final newline exists after line ending conversion.
Directory names to exclude from conversion (e.g., .git, bin, obj).
File patterns to exclude from conversion.
Export the detailed report to a CSV file at the specified path.
Convert encoding inconsistencies.
Convert line ending inconsistencies.
Force conversion even when the file already matches the target.
Per-path line ending overrides (hashtable of pattern => line ending).
Do not rollback from backup if verification mismatch occurs during encoding conversion.
Only fix files missing the final newline.
Only convert files that have mixed line endings.
Path to the project directory to convert.
Type of project to analyze. Determines which file extensions are included.
Target encoding to enforce when fixing encoding consistency.
Target line ending style to enforce when fixing line endings.
Include detailed file-by-file analysis in the output.
Source encoding filter. When Any, any non-target encoding may be converted.

maml:description.maml:para: Finds module versions from a managed module repository.

This command is the module-package equivalent of Find-PSResource. It queries NuGet-compatible or local-folder repositories through the managed C# repository client and returns typed module version objects that can be piped to Install-ManagedModule or Save-ManagedModule.

Name, exact/range version, prerelease, tag, and dependency searches are supported for module packages. Command-name and DSC-resource-name searches inspect package contents and remain outside this fast metadata path.
Return all matching versions instead of only the latest selected version.
Optional repository credential.
Optional repository credential secret.
Optional path to a file containing the repository credential secret.
Optional repository credential username.
Maximum search results returned for wildcard name queries.
Include dependency resources exposed by repository metadata.
Module names or wildcard patterns to find. When omitted, all module package names are considered.
Include prerelease versions.
Saved module repository profile to use instead of Repository.
Optional HTTP proxy used for repository requests.
Optional proxy credential used with Proxy.
Repository URL, NuGet v3 service index, flat-container URL, or local folder feed.
Friendly repository name used in output.
Resource kind to find. Find-ManagedModule currently returns module resources.
Filter results by package tag metadata.
Exact version, wildcard version, or NuGet-style version range to return.
Return all matching versions instead of only the latest selected version.
Optional repository credential.
Optional repository credential secret.
Optional path to a file containing the repository credential secret.
Optional repository credential username.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets, tests, or exports saved managed module repository profiles.

Repository profiles contain non-secret feed settings. Use this cmdlet to review repository shape, test local onboarding readiness, or export profile definitions for another machine without creating another command family.

Overwrite an existing export file.
Optional repository profile names. When omitted, all visible profiles are returned.
Return profile objects after exporting.
Profile store scope to read. The default reads user profiles first, then machine-wide profiles.
Return readiness information instead of profile definitions.
Optional destination JSON file for exporting the selected profiles.
Overwrite an existing export file.
Optional repository profile names. When omitted, all visible profiles are returned.
Return profile objects after exporting.
Profile store scope to read. The default reads user profiles first, then machine-wide profiles.
Return readiness information instead of profile definitions.
Readiness information for a saved private module repository profile.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets module documentation (README, CHANGELOG, LICENSE, Intro/Upgrade) and renders it in the console.

Resolves documentation files from an installed module (root or Internals folder) and renders them with Spectre.Console. When local files are absent, it will backfill from the module's repository specified by PrivateData.PSData.ProjectUri (GitHub or Azure DevOps), using a token when necessary.

Use it in build scripts to avoid re-implementing manifest discovery logic.

maml:description.maml:para: Analyzes PowerShell files and folders to determine compatibility with Windows PowerShell 5.1 and PowerShell 7+.
Scans PowerShell files to detect patterns and constructs that can cause cross-version issues (Windows PowerShell 5.1 vs PowerShell 7+), and outputs a compatibility report.
Use this as part of CI to keep modules compatible across editions, and pair it with encoding/line-ending checks when supporting Windows PowerShell 5.1.

When analyzing a directory, recursively analyze all subdirectories.
Include detailed analysis of each file with specific compatibility issues found.

This cmdlet combines encoding and line-ending analysis to provide a single “consistency” view of your repository. It is intended to be run before bulk conversions (encoding/line endings) and before packaging a module for release.
For fixing issues during builds, use New-ConfigurationFileConsistency with -AutoFix enabled.

maml:description.maml:para: Imports a known PowerShell module through a curated AssemblyLoadContext isolation profile.

Import-IsolatedModule is intended for PowerShell 7+ sessions where one service module's binary dependencies cannot safely share the default AssemblyLoadContext with another module. The command resolves the selected profile, copies the target module to a temporary working folder, generates a profile-specific wrapper, loads selected binary assemblies through a module-scoped AssemblyLoadContext, and imports the generated wrapper into the current runspace.

Built-in profiles are maintained by PowerForge and are deliberately curated. Use the ExchangeOnlineManagement profile when Exchange Online needs to keep its binary surface out of the default context. Use the MicrosoftTeams profile when Teams should keep its MSAL, IdentityModel, policy administration, and ConfigAPI binary stack isolated. Use the MicrosoftGraphAuthentication profile when Graph authentication assemblies should be loaded through a profile-specific context.

This command does not authenticate to the isolated module. After importing the profile, call the target module's normal connection command, such as Connect-ExchangeOnline or Connect-MicrosoftTeams. Windows PowerShell 5.1 is not supported because AssemblyLoadContext is only available on CoreCLR.
Use this only when testing a compatible module installed under a non-standard module name. The selected profile still controls script patching, binary imports, and the load-context name.
The result includes the source module path, generated script and manifest paths, import path, work root, load-context name, and profile counts.
Use Path to test a specific installed version or a copied module payload. Directory paths are treated as module bases. File paths are resolved to their parent directory.

When set, Import-IsolatedModule prepends the generated module parent directory to PSModulePath after the isolated import succeeds. Later Import-Module calls and RequiredModules resolution in the same PowerShell process can then find the isolated copy before the original installed module. This is session-scoped and intentionally opt-in because it changes module-name resolution for the process.
Supported built-in profiles are ExchangeOnlineManagement, MicrosoftTeams, and MicrosoftGraphAuthentication. Profile names are resolved case-insensitively.

When omitted, generated module copies are created under the system temp folder in PowerForge\IsolatedModules\<profile>. Use WorkRoot when you want to inspect generated wrappers or keep diagnostic artifacts in a known location.
Use this only when testing a compatible module installed under a non-standard module name. The selected profile still controls script patching, binary imports, and the load-context name.
The result includes the source module path, generated script and manifest paths, import path, work root, load-context name, and profile counts.
Use Path to test a specific installed version or a copied module payload. Directory paths are treated as module bases. File paths are resolved to their parent directory.

When set, Import-IsolatedModule prepends the generated module parent directory to PSModulePath after the isolated import succeeds. Later Import-Module calls and RequiredModules resolution in the same PowerShell process can then find the isolated copy before the original installed module. This is session-scoped and intentionally opt-in because it changes module-name resolution for the process.
Supported built-in profiles are ExchangeOnlineManagement, MicrosoftTeams, and MicrosoftGraphAuthentication. Profile names are resolved case-insensitively.

When omitted, generated module copies are created under the system temp folder in PowerForge\IsolatedModules\<profile>. Use WorkRoot when you want to inspect generated wrappers or keep diagnostic artifacts in a known location.

command:example.maml:title: Start an isolated Exchange Online session
command:example.dev:code: Import-IsolatedModule -Profile ExchangeOnlineManagement $commands = 'Get-EXOMailbox', 'Get-ConnectionInformation' Connect-ExchangeOnline -ShowBanner:$false -CommandName $commands Get-ConnectionInformation | Format-Table UserPrincipalName, ConnectionUri Get-EXOMailbox -ResultSize 5 | Select-Object DisplayName, PrimarySmtpAddress Disconnect-ExchangeOnline -Confirm:$false

dev:remarks.maml:para: Imports ExchangeOnlineManagement through the curated profile, connects with the normal EXO cmdlets, runs a small mailbox query, and disconnects cleanly.

command:example.maml:title: Use Exchange Online after Az.Storage has loaded OData 7.6
command:example.dev:code: Import-Module Az.Storage $defaultODataBefore = [System.Runtime.Loader.AssemblyLoadContext]::Default.Assemblies | Where-Object { $_.GetName().Name -like 'Microsoft.OData*' } Import-IsolatedModule -Profile ExchangeOnlineManagement Connect-ExchangeOnline -ShowBanner:$false $defaultODataBefore | ForEach-Object { $_.GetName().Name + ' ' + $_.GetName().Version } Get-EXOMailbox -ResultSize 1 | Select-Object DisplayName, PrimarySmtpAddress Disconnect-ExchangeOnline -Confirm:$false

dev:remarks.maml:para: Keeps Az.Storage's Microsoft.OData 7.6 assemblies in the default context while Exchange Online loads Microsoft.OData 7.22 assemblies in ExchangeOnlineManagement.ALC.

command:example.maml:title: Start an isolated Microsoft Teams session
command:example.dev:code: Import-IsolatedModule -Profile MicrosoftTeams Connect-MicrosoftTeams -UseDeviceAuthentication $teams = Get-Team $teams | Select-Object -First 10 DisplayName, GroupId, Visibility Disconnect-MicrosoftTeams

dev:remarks.maml:para: Imports Teams cmdlets from MicrosoftTeams.ALC and then uses the normal Teams connection workflow.

command:example.maml:title: Inspect the generated isolated Teams module
command:example.dev:code: $result = Import-IsolatedModule -Profile MicrosoftTeams -PassThru $result | Format-List ProfileName, ContextName, IsolatedImportPath, IsolatedScriptPath, WorkPath Get-Command -Module MicrosoftTeams.ALC | Measure-Object [System.Runtime.Loader.AssemblyLoadContext]::All | Where-Object Name -eq $result.ContextName | ForEach-Object { $_.Assemblies | Where-Object { $_.GetName().Name -like 'Microsoft.Teams*' } | Select-Object -ExpandProperty FullName }

dev:remarks.maml:para: Returns the generated wrapper details and confirms that Teams assemblies were loaded in the profile-specific AssemblyLoadContext.

command:example.maml:title: Import a specific installed module copy by path
command:example.dev:code: $module = Get-Module -ListAvailable ExchangeOnlineManagement | Sort-Object Version -Descending | Select-Object -First 1 $result = Import-IsolatedModule -Profile ExchangeOnlineManagement -Path $module.ModuleBase -PassThru $result | Select-Object ProfileName, ModuleName, SourceModuleBase, IsolatedImportPath Get-Module ExchangeOnlineManagement | Select-Object Name, Version, ModuleBase

dev:remarks.maml:para: Uses the profile rules but bypasses PSModulePath discovery by pointing at a specific module base folder. The profile still validates its minimum supported module version.

command:example.maml:title: Use a deterministic work root for diagnostics
command:example.dev:code: $workRoot = Join-Path $env:TEMP 'PowerForge-Isolated' $result = Import-IsolatedModule -Profile MicrosoftTeams -WorkRoot $workRoot -PassThru Get-ChildItem -LiteralPath $result.WorkPath -Recurse | Select-Object -First 20 FullName Get-Content -LiteralPath $result.IsolatedScriptPath -TotalCount 40

dev:remarks.maml:para: Creates the generated module copy under the supplied root and inspects the generated wrapper.

command:example.maml:title: Prefer the generated isolated copy for later module imports
command:example.dev:code: Import-Module Az.Storage $result = Import-IsolatedModule -Profile ExchangeOnlineManagement -PreferIsolatedModulePath -PassThru Connect-ExchangeOnline -ShowBanner:$false Import-Module Contoso.ExchangeWorker Invoke-ContosoExchangeWorker $result | Format-List IsolatedModuleResolutionPath, PreferIsolatedModulePath Disconnect-ExchangeOnline -Confirm:$false

dev:remarks.maml:para: Prepends the generated isolated module parent path to PSModulePath for the current process. This is useful when a downstream module imports ExchangeOnlineManagement by name and should bind to the isolated copy instead of the original installed module.

command:example.maml:title: Preview an import without creating the isolated module copy

dev:remarks.maml:para: Uses ShouldProcess support to show the intended operation without preparing or importing the wrapper.

maml:description.maml:para: Performs one-command onboarding for managed module repository profiles.

This cmdlet is the workstation or build-agent onboarding entry point for managed module repositories. It can use an existing saved profile, import a non-secret profile JSON file, or create a profile from feed details. Unless -SkipConnect is used, it then installs requested prerequisites, registers/probes native provider state where needed, and validates authenticated access through the selected bootstrap mode. It can also write a distributable non-secret bootstrap package for other machines.
Overwrite existing bootstrap files when BootstrapPath is used.
Optional output directory for a non-secret onboarding package that imports the selected profiles and can install starter modules.
Generated profile JSON file name when BootstrapPath is used.
Generated bootstrap script file name when BootstrapPath is used.
Optional repository credential secret for credential-prompt fallback environments.
Optional path to a file containing the repository credential secret.
Optional repository credential username for credential-prompt fallback environments.
Optional module names written into the bootstrap script as starter managed installs.
Installs missing private-gallery prerequisites before connecting, including the PSResourceGet version required by the selected bootstrap mode and the Azure Artifacts credential provider.
Saved repository profile name. When used with Path, selects one imported profile from the file. When used with Azure Artifacts feed details, creates that profile name.
Prompts interactively for repository credentials in credential-prompt fallback environments.
Profile store scope. Existing profiles default to user-then-machine lookup; profile creation/import defaults to the current user's store unless Machine is specified.

maml:description.maml:para: Installs PowerShell modules through the managed C# module engine.

This command provides the module-install functionality of Install-PSResource through the PowerForge repository, dependency, integrity, and promotion services. It accepts names, required-resource maps, required resource files, and typed output from Find-ManagedModule without invoking PowerShellGet or PSResourceGet.
Managed installs always return a typed result or plan. Repository trust is explicit through managed profiles and trust policies instead of a one-invocation prompt-suppression switch.
Accept package licenses when packages declare license acceptance is required.
Allow command exports to overlap with other modules in the target root.
Allowed package author values from package metadata.
Validate Authenticode signatures for signable package files before promotion.
Optional repository credential.
Optional repository credential secret.
Optional path to a file containing the repository credential secret.
Optional repository credential username.
Maximum number of dependency branches to install concurrently. Omit to use the managed engine default.
Expected SHA256 hash of the root package before it is extracted and promoted.
Reinstall the module version when it already exists.
Maximum package version to install when Version is omitted.
Minimum package version to install when Version is omitted.
Explicit module root. Use with Scope Custom.
Module names to install.
PSResourceGet-compatible spelling for the managed default that rejects command export conflicts.
Optional package cache directory.
Return an inspectable install plan without writing files.
Include prerelease versions when resolving the latest version.

Optional HTTP proxy used for repository requests.

Require the selected repository profile to be marked trusted.

Write a compact Spectre.Console summary for each plan or result.
Skip installing dependencies declared by the package.

maml:description.maml:para: Installs a module and its embedded dependencies to an explicit private runtime folder.

The command reads the dependency manifest produced by New-ConfigurationModule -Type EmbeddedModule and copies the root module plus each dependency payload to the requested path. Modules are not installed into PSModulePath unless the chosen path is already part of PSModulePath.

Overwrite existing dependency folders when merge or overwrite behavior requires it.
Preview planned dependency copies without writing files.

Conflict handling when a dependency version folder already exists.
Destination folder. The root module and dependencies are copied under Name\Version folders.

maml:description.maml:para: Copies a module's bundled documentation (Internals, README/CHANGELOG/LICENSE) to a chosen location.

Resolves the module and copies its documentation payload into a destination folder arranged by DocumentationLayout. The payload is the module's delivery Internals folder (or the default Internals folder) plus selected root documentation files such as README, CHANGELOG and LICENSE. Repeat runs can merge, refresh, overwrite, skip or stop based on OnExistsOption. The default Merge mode adds missing files and keeps existing files unless -Force is used. The Refresh mode overwrites package files while keeping local files that are not part of the package. When successful, returns the destination path.
Copies a module's bundled documentation (Internals, README/CHANGELOG/LICENSE) to a chosen location.

Resolves the module and copies its documentation payload into a destination folder arranged by DocumentationLayout. The payload is the module's delivery Internals folder (or the default Internals folder) plus selected root documentation files such as README, CHANGELOG and LICENSE. Repeat runs can merge, refresh, overwrite, skip or stop based on OnExistsOption. The default Merge mode adds missing files and keeps existing files unless -Force is used. The Refresh mode overwrites package files while keeping local files that are not part of the package. When successful, returns the destination path.
Legacy toggle equivalent to selecting ModuleAndVersion when set; Direct when not set.
Allow replacement of existing files during merge, and clear read-only attributes when overwrite needs to delete an existing destination.
Output folder structure strategy. The default, ModuleAndVersion, keeps each module version in its own subfolder.
Plan only; output the resolved destination path without copying files or changing the destination.
Module name to install documentation for.
Suppress delivery IntroText or IntroFile output during installation.

Behavior when the destination folder already exists. The default, Merge, adds missing files and preserves existing files unless -Force is used. Refresh overwrites package files without deleting unrelated local files.
Open the resulting folder or README after installation.
Base destination folder where documentation will be written. The final folder also depends on Layout.
Exact version to select when multiple module versions are installed.
Legacy toggle equivalent to selecting ModuleAndVersion when set; Direct when not set.
Allow replacement of existing files during merge, and clear read-only attributes when overwrite needs to delete an existing destination.
Output folder structure strategy. The default, ModuleAndVersion, keeps each module version in its own subfolder.

maml:description.maml:para: Executes DotNet publish engine from DSL settings or an existing JSON config.
This cmdlet follows the same authoring pattern as module build cmdlets: create a config using New-ConfigurationDotNet* and run it directly, or export config first via -JsonOnly + -JsonPath.

maml:description.maml:para: Builds a .NET project in Release configuration and prepares release artefacts.

The cmdlet discovers the .csproj file (when a directory is provided), reads VersionPrefix from the project, then runs dotnet build and dotnet pack in Release (by default). It produces a ZIP snapshot of the release output and returns a typed result object for each input project path.
Use -WhatIf to preview the planned outputs without running build/pack/sign operations.
Optional certificate thumbprint used to sign assemblies and packages. When omitted, no signing is performed.
Certificate store location used when searching for the signing certificate. Default: CurrentUser.
When enabled, also packs all project dependencies that have their own .csproj files.
Path to the folder containing the project (*.csproj) file (or the csproj file itself).
Timestamp server URL used while signing. Default: http://timestamp.digicert.com.
Optional certificate thumbprint used to sign assemblies and packages. When omitted, no signing is performed.
Certificate store location used when searching for the signing certificate. Default: CurrentUser.
When enabled, also packs all project dependencies that have their own .csproj files.
Path to the folder containing the project (*.csproj) file (or the csproj file itself).
Timestamp server URL used while signing. Default: http://timestamp.digicert.com.

maml:description.maml:para: Repository-wide .NET package release workflow (discover, version, pack, publish).
Discovers packable projects, resolves a repository-wide version (supports X-pattern), updates csproj versions, packs, and optionally publishes packages.
Align projects sharing an X-pattern to one next version based on the highest current package version in that group.

maml:description.maml:para: Creates/updates a module structure and triggers the build pipeline (legacy DSL compatible).

This is the primary entry point for building a PowerShell module using PSPublishModule. Configuration is provided via a DSL using New-Configuration* cmdlets (typically inside the -Settings scriptblock) and then executed by the PowerForge pipeline runner.
To generate a reusable powerforge.json configuration file (for the PowerForge CLI) without running any build steps, use -JsonOnly with -JsonPath.
When running in an interactive terminal, pipeline execution uses a Spectre.Console progress UI. Redirect output or use -Verbose to force plain, line-by-line output (useful for CI logs).

Dependency behavior is composed from the configuration segments you emit. Typically this means: New-ConfigurationModule declares dependencies, New-ConfigurationBuild decides whether the build host should install missing ones, and New-ConfigurationArtefact decides whether required modules should be bundled into the output artefact.
Folder name containing aliases to export. Default: Public.
Optional path to a .NET project (.csproj) to publish into the module.
Optional path to a diagnostics baseline file used to compare current issues with known issues.
Optional module roots to scan for deterministic binary conflict diagnostics. When provided, conflict findings can participate in diagnostics baselines and policy.
Build configuration for publishing the .NET project (Release or Debug).
Target frameworks to publish (e.g., net472, net8.0).
Directory names excluded from staging copy (matched by directory name, not by path).
File names excluded from staging copy (matched by file name, not by path).
Exclude patterns for artefact packaging.
When specified, requests the host to exit with code 0 on success and 1 on failure.
Fails the build when diagnostics at or above the specified severity are present.
Fails the build when diagnostics appear that are not present in the loaded baseline.
Folder name containing functions to export. Default: Public.
Writes a diagnostics baseline file from the current run.
Folders from which to include all files in artefacts.
Optional script block executed during staging that can add custom files/folders to the build.
Folders from which to include .ps1 files in artefacts.
Include patterns for root files in artefacts.
Advanced hashtable form for includes (maps IncludeRoot/IncludePS1/IncludeAll etc).
Destination module roots for install. When omitted, defaults are used.
Installation strategy used when installing the module.
Generates a PowerForge pipeline JSON file and exits without running the build pipeline. Intended for migrating legacy DSL scripts to powerforge CLI configuration.
Output path for the generated pipeline JSON file (used with JsonOnly). Defaults to powerforge.json in the project root.
Keep staging directory after build/install.
Number of versions to keep per module root when installing.
Compatibility switch. Historically forced the PowerShell-script build pipeline; the build now always runs through the C# PowerForge pipeline.
How to handle legacy flat installs found under module roots.
Alternate relative path for .NET Core-targeted libraries folder. Default: Lib/Core.
Alternate relative path for .NET Framework-targeted libraries folder. Default: Lib/Default.
Alternate relative path for .NET Standard-targeted libraries folder. Default: Lib/Standard.
Name of the module being built.
Disables the interactive progress UI and emits plain log output.
Path to the parent folder where the project exists or should be created. The module project resolves to Path\ModuleName. When omitted, uses the parent of the calling script directory.
Version folders to preserve when pruning installed versions.

maml:description.maml:para: Exports plugin folders from a reusable PowerForge plugin catalog configuration.

This cmdlet is the PowerShell entry point for the same generic plugin export engine used by powerforge plugin export. It plans and optionally publishes plugin project outputs into folder-based plugin bundles without hardcoding IntelligenceX-specific project lists or manifest formats into the engine.
Path to the plugin catalog configuration file. When omitted, the cmdlet searches the current directory and its parents for standard PowerForge plugin config names.
Optional configuration override.
Sets host exit code: 0 on success, 1 on failure.
Optional plugin group filter. When omitted, all catalog entries are selected.
Keeps symbol files in exported plugin folders.
Optional output root override for exported plugin folders.
Builds and emits the resolved export plan without executing dotnet publish.
Optional preferred framework override used when a catalog entry targets multiple frameworks.
Optional project root override for resolving plugin project paths.

Optional project root override for resolving plugin project paths.

maml:description.maml:para: Packs plugin-related NuGet packages from a reusable PowerForge plugin catalog configuration.

This cmdlet is the PowerShell entry point for the same generic plugin package workflow used by powerforge plugin pack. It selects package groups from the shared catalog, runs dotnet pack, and can optionally push only the packages produced by the current run.

maml:description.maml:para: Executes the unified repository release workflow from a JSON configuration.

This cmdlet is the PowerShell entry point for the same unified release engine used by powerforge release. It can coordinate repository package publishing and downloadable tool/app artefacts from one configuration file.

maml:description.maml:para: Executes a repository-wide .NET build/release pipeline from a JSON configuration.

The configuration file follows Schemas/project.build.schema.json in the PSPublishModule repository. Use it to define discovery rules, versioning, staging, NuGet publishing, and GitHub release settings.

GitHub tag/release templates support tokens: {Project}, {Version}, {PrimaryProject}, {PrimaryVersion}, {Repo}, {Repository}, {Date}, {UtcDate}, {DateTime}, {UtcDateTime}, {Timestamp}, {UtcTimestamp}. When GitHub release mode is Single and multiple project versions are present, {Version} defaults to the local date (yyyy.MM.dd) unless a primary project version is available.

command:example.maml:title: Prepare an iOS app using the resolved pipeline version
command:example.dev:code: New-ConfigurationAppleApp -Name Tactra -Platform iOS -ProjectPath .\Tactra.xcodeproj -Scheme Tactra -BundleId com.example.Tactra -UseResolvedVersion -BuildNumberPolicy IncrementExisting

dev:remarks.maml:para: Sets MARKETING_VERSION from the pipeline version and increments CURRENT_PROJECT_VERSION.

maml:description.maml:para: Tells the module to create an artefact of a specified type.
Artefacts are created after the module is built into staging. Use Packed (ZIP) for distribution and Unpacked (folder) for inspection or offline installation.

When -AddRequiredModules is enabled, required modules are copied from locally available modules (Get-Module -ListAvailable) and, when configured, downloaded (via Save-PSResource/Save-Module) before being copied into the artefact.

Only RequiredModule dependencies participate in artefact bundling. ExternalModule dependencies are intentionally excluded because they represent dependencies that should remain separately installed on the target machine.

RequiredModulesSource controls the packaging strategy: Installed means local-only copy, Auto means prefer local and download when missing, and Download means always download. When omitted, the default is Installed.
Use -ID to link an artefact to a publish step (New-ConfigurationPublish) and publish only a specific artefact.
For a broader dependency workflow explanation, see about_ModuleDependencies.
Add RequiredModule dependencies to the artefact by copying or downloading them. This does not include ExternalModule dependencies.
The name of the artefact. If not specified, the default name will be used.
Directories to copy to artefact (Source/Destination). Accepts legacy hashtable (source=>destination) or ArtefactCopyMapping[]
Define if destination directories should be relative to artefact root.
Files to copy to artefact (Source/Destination). Accepts legacy hashtable (source=>destination) or ArtefactCopyMapping[]
Define if destination files should be relative to artefact root.
Do not clear artefact output directory before creating artefact.
Enable artefact creation. By default artefact creation is disabled.
Optional ID of the artefact (to be used by New-ConfigurationPublish).
Include tag name in artefact name. By default tag name is not included.
Path where main module (or required module) will be copied to.
Path where artefact will be created.
ScriptBlock that will be added at the end of the script (Script / ScriptPacked).
Path to file that will be added at the end of the script (Script / ScriptPacked).
ScriptBlock that will be added at the beginning of the script (Script / ScriptPacked).
Path to file that will be added at the beginning of the script (Script / ScriptPacked).
Repository credential secret (password/token) in clear text used when downloading required modules.
Repository credential secret (password/token) in a clear-text file used when downloading required modules.
Repository credential username (basic auth) used when downloading required modules.
Required module names that should not be copied into this artefact when -AddRequiredModules is enabled. This only affects artefact bundling; it does not remove modules from the generated manifest.
Path where required modules will be copied to. When omitted, PowerForge uses the default module layout under the artefact output.
Repository name used when downloading required modules (Save-PSResource / Save-Module). Set this when packaging should resolve from a specific gallery or private feed.

Source used when resolving required modules (Auto / Installed / Download). When omitted, PowerForge defaults to Installed, which means packaging expects the dependency to already exist on the machine.
Tool used when downloading required modules (Save-PSResource / Save-Module). Auto prefers PSResourceGet and falls back to PowerShellGet when necessary.
The name of the script artefact (alias: FileName).
Artefact type to generate.

maml:description.maml:para: Allows configuring the build process for a module.

This cmdlet emits build configuration that is consumed by Invoke-ModuleBuild / Build-Module. It controls how the module is merged, signed, versioned, installed, and how optional .NET publishing is performed.

Dependency-related options in this cmdlet affect the build machine, not artefact packaging. Use InstallMissingModules when the build host needs missing RequiredModule or ExternalModule dependencies installed before merge/import/test steps run.

If you want dependencies copied into ZIP/unpacked artefacts, configure that separately with New-ConfigurationArtefact -AddRequiredModules. Build-time installation and artefact packaging are designed as separate decisions because many teams want one without the other.
For a broader dependency workflow explanation, see about_ModuleDependencies.
Auto switch VersionedInstallStrategy to Exact when publishing.
Base64 string of a PFX containing a code-signing certificate.
Password for the PFX provided via CertificatePFXPath or CertificatePFXBase64.
Path to a PFX containing a code-signing certificate.
Thumbprint of a code-signing certificate from the local cert store.
Delete target module before build.
Do not attempt to fix relative paths during merge.
Keep classes in a separate dot-sourced file instead of merging into the main PSM1.
Keep library-loading code in a separate dot-sourced file.
Enable build process.
Install missing module dependencies (RequiredModule/ExternalModule) before build. This affects the build host only; it does not bundle modules into artefacts.
Credential secret or token for dependency installation. Prefer the file-path form in CI when you do not want the secret value embedded directly in scripts.
Path to a file containing the credential secret or token. This is often the safest option for automation and CI agents.
Credential user name for dependency installation. This is usually paired with InstallMissingModulesCredentialSecret or InstallMissingModulesCredentialSecretFilePath.
Force re-install or update even if dependencies are already installed. Useful when you want the build host to re-sync against the repository instead of accepting the current local state.
Allow prerelease versions when installing dependencies. Use this only when the dependency declaration and repository policy intentionally allow prerelease packages.
Repository name used for dependency installation (defaults to PSGallery). Set this when your build should resolve dependencies from a named private feed or alternate gallery.
Kill locking processes before install.
Force killing locking processes before install.
Use local versioning (bump PSD1 version on each build without querying PSGallery).
When merging, also include functions from ApprovedModules referenced by the module.
Merge module on build (combine Private/Public/Classes/Enums into one PSM1).
Load the binary module through a custom AssemblyLoadContext on PowerShell Core.
Assembly simple names whose public types may be exposed as PowerShell type accelerators when assembly mode is enabled.
Controls optional type accelerator exposure for dependency types loaded in the module AssemblyLoadContext.
Fully-qualified dependency type names to expose as PowerShell type accelerators from the module AssemblyLoadContext.
Binary module names (DLL file names) to import in the module.
Disable cmdlet scanning for the binary module.
Enable binary module documentation.
Build configuration for .NET projects (Release or Debug).
Generate checked-in source bootstrapper logic for local development binary outputs.
Optional environment variable used by Environment development-binary mode.
Controls when the generated source bootstrapper loads local development binaries.
Optional root folder that contains configuration/framework development binary outputs.
Optional environment variable that chooses the development build configuration.
Controls how the source PSM1 is maintained when development binary bootstrapping is enabled.
Do not copy libraries recursively (legacy option).
Filters for libraries that should be excluded from build output.
Exclude main library from build output.
Target frameworks for .NET build.
Handle 'assembly with same name is already loaded' by wrapping Add-Type logic.
Handle runtimes folder when copying libraries.
Exclude libraries from being loaded by PSM1/Libraries.ps1.
Add-Type libraries line by line (legacy debugging option).
Debug DLL merge (legacy setting).
Project name for the .NET project (required when NETProjectPath is provided).
Path to the .NET project to build (useful when not in Sources folder).
Enable resolving binary conflicts.

Search class (legacy option).
Only regenerate the manifest (PSD1) without rebuilding/merging other artefacts.
Resolve Auto/Latest dependency versions from the repository without installing. When not explicitly set, this is auto-enabled if any RequiredModules use Auto/Latest/Guid Auto.
Write library-loading code into a distinct file and reference it via ScriptsToProcess/DotSource.
Override include patterns passed to the signer.
Additional path substrings to exclude from signing.
When signing is enabled, binaries are signed by default (e.g., .dll, .cat). Use -SignIncludeBinaries:$false to opt out.
When signing is enabled, include .exe files in signing.
When signing is enabled, also sign scripts that reside under the Internals folder.
Enable code-signing for the built module output.
When signing is enabled, overwrite existing signatures (re-sign files).
Disables built-in replacements done by the module builder.
Synchronize the source .NET project version with the resolved module/manifest version before staging. This is opt-in and updates the source .csproj file when a project path can be resolved.
Export all functions/aliases via wildcard in PSD1.
How many versions to keep per module when using versioned installs.
How to handle legacy flat module installs during install.

Controls how the module is installed into user Module roots after build.
Warn if RequiredModule entries are older than the latest version available in the repository. This is a reporting hint and does not change the manifest or install anything by itself.

Credential secret or token for dependency installation. Prefer the file-path form in CI when you do not want the secret value embedded directly in scripts.
Path to a file containing the credential secret or token. This is often the safest option for automation and CI agents.
Credential user name for dependency installation. This is usually paired with InstallMissingModulesCredentialSecret or InstallMissingModulesCredentialSecretFilePath.
Force re-install or update even if dependencies are already installed. Useful when you want the build host to re-sync against the repository instead of accepting the current local state.
Allow prerelease versions when installing dependencies. Use this only when the dependency declaration and repository policy intentionally allow prerelease packages.
Repository name used for dependency installation (defaults to PSGallery). Set this when your build should resolve dependencies from a named private feed or alternate gallery.
Kill locking processes before install.
Force killing locking processes before install.
Use local versioning (bump PSD1 version on each build without querying PSGallery).
When merging, also include functions from ApprovedModules referenced by the module.
Merge module on build (combine Private/Public/Classes/Enums into one PSM1).
Load the binary module through a custom AssemblyLoadContext on PowerShell Core.
Assembly simple names whose public types may be exposed as PowerShell type accelerators when assembly mode is enabled.
Controls optional type accelerator exposure for dependency types loaded in the module AssemblyLoadContext.
Fully-qualified dependency type names to expose as PowerShell type accelerators from the module AssemblyLoadContext.
Binary module names (DLL file names) to import in the module.
Disable cmdlet scanning for the binary module.
Enable binary module documentation.
Build configuration for .NET projects (Release or Debug).
Generate checked-in source bootstrapper logic for local development binary outputs.
Optional environment variable used by Environment development-binary mode.
Controls when the generated source bootstrapper loads local development binaries.
Optional root folder that contains configuration/framework development binary outputs.
Optional environment variable that chooses the development build configuration.
Controls how the source PSM1 is maintained when development binary bootstrapping is enabled.
Do not copy libraries recursively (legacy option).
Filters for libraries that should be excluded from build output.
Exclude main library from build output.
Target frameworks for .NET build.
Handle 'assembly with same name is already loaded' by wrapping Add-Type logic.
Handle runtimes folder when copying libraries.
Exclude libraries from being loaded by PSM1/Libraries.ps1.
Add-Type libraries line by line (legacy debugging option).
Debug DLL merge (legacy setting).
Project name for the .NET project (required when NETProjectPath is provided).
Path to the .NET project to build (useful when not in Sources folder).
Enable resolving binary conflicts.
Project name used when resolving binary conflicts.
Search class (legacy option).
Only regenerate the manifest (PSD1) without rebuilding/merging other artefacts.
Resolve Auto/Latest dependency versions from the repository without installing. When not explicitly set, this is auto-enabled if any RequiredModules use Auto/Latest/Guid Auto.
Write library-loading code into a distinct file and reference it via ScriptsToProcess/DotSource.
Override include patterns passed to the signer.
Additional path substrings to exclude from signing.
When signing is enabled, binaries are signed by default (e.g., .dll, .cat). Use -SignIncludeBinaries:$false to opt out.
When signing is enabled, include .exe files in signing.
When signing is enabled, also sign scripts that reside under the Internals folder.
Enable code-signing for the built module output.
When signing is enabled, overwrite existing signatures (re-sign files).
Disables built-in replacements done by the module builder.
Synchronize the source .NET project version with the resolved module/manifest version before staging. This is opt-in and updates the source .csproj file when a project path can be resolved.
Export all functions/aliases via wildcard in PSD1.
How many versions to keep per module when using versioned installs.
How to handle legacy flat module installs during install.
Version folders to preserve during install pruning (for example older major versions).
Controls how the module is installed into user Module roots after build.
Warn if RequiredModule entries are older than the latest version available in the repository. This is a reporting hint and does not change the manifest or install anything by itself.

maml:description.maml:para: Configures delivery metadata for bundling and installing internal docs/examples.

Delivery configuration is used to bundle “internals” (docs, examples, tools, configuration files) into a module and optionally generate public helper commands (Install-<ModuleName> / Update-<ModuleName>) that can copy these files to a target folder.
This is intended for “script packages” where the module contains additional artifacts that should be deployed alongside it.

Generated install helpers default to merge behavior. Generated update helpers default to refresh behavior, which overwrites package files while keeping local files that are not part of the package. Use IncludePaths and ExcludePaths to scope which package files are managed, then use PreservePaths and OverwritePaths to fine-tune collision behavior for managed relative paths.
Where to bundle CHANGELOG.* within the built module.
Optional file-name order for Internals\\Docs when rendering documentation.
Enables delivery metadata emission.

Optional wildcard patterns (relative to InternalsPath) that generated Install-/Update- helpers should not copy, refresh, or consider package-owned. Exclusions win over IncludePaths. Use this for local-only or user-owned package-adjacent paths such as Config/local/** or Config/config.json.
When set, generates a public Install-<ModuleName> helper function during build that copies Internals to a destination folder.
When set, generates a public Update-<ModuleName> helper function during build that delegates to the install command in refresh mode by default.
Important links (Title/Url). Accepts legacy hashtable array (@{ Title='..'; Url='..' }) or DeliveryImportantLink[].

Optional wildcard patterns (relative to InternalsPath) that define which package files generated Install-/Update- helpers manage. Use this when a module ships mixed content but only selected folders should be copied or refreshed, for example Config/*.sample.json and Scripts/*.ps1. When omitted, all files under InternalsPath are managed.
Include module root CHANGELOG.* during installation.
Include module root LICENSE.* during installation.
Include module root README.* during installation.
Optional override name for the generated install command. When empty, defaults to Install-<ModuleName>.
Relative path inside the module that contains internal deliverables.
Relative path (within the module root) to a Markdown/text file to use as Intro content.
Text lines shown to users after Install-ModuleDocumentation completes.
Where to bundle LICENSE.* within the built module.
Optional wildcard patterns (relative to Internals) that should be overwritten during merge installs by generated Install-/Update- helpers. Example: Artefacts/**.
Optional wildcard patterns (relative to Internals) that should be preserved during merge or refresh installs by generated Install-/Update- helpers. Example: Config/**.
Where to bundle README.* within the built module.
Optional branch name to use when fetching remote documentation.
One or more repository-relative paths from which to display remote documentation files.
When set, requests signing for files under InternalsPath using the configured module signing settings/certificate.
Optional override name for the generated update command. When empty, defaults to Update-<ModuleName>.
Relative path (within the module root) to a Markdown/text file to use for Upgrade instructions.
Text lines with upgrade instructions shown when requested.
Where to bundle CHANGELOG.* within the built module.
Optional file-name order for Internals\\Docs when rendering documentation.

When set, generates a public Install-<ModuleName> helper function during build that copies Internals to a destination folder.
When set, generates a public Update-<ModuleName> helper function during build that delegates to the install command in refresh mode by default.

When omitted, all files under InternalsPath are managed.

maml:description.maml:para: Enables or disables creation of documentation from the module using PowerForge.

This cmdlet emits documentation configuration segments that are consumed by Invoke-ModuleBuild / Build-Module. It controls markdown generation (in -Path), optional external help generation (MAML, e.g. en-US\<ModuleName>-help.xml), and project-root documentation sync. When -Enable is used, PowerForge generates documentation, cleans stale files in the configured docs folder, and updates the project documentation path by default.

About topics are supported via about_*.help.txt / about_*.txt / about_*.md / about_*.markdown files present in the module source. When enabled, these are converted into markdown pages under Docs\About. Additional source roots can be provided via AboutTopicsSourcePath.
Optional extra source paths for about_* topic files (.help.txt, .txt, .md, .markdown). Relative paths are resolved from the staging root (for example: 'Help\About').
Enables creation of documentation from the module.
Culture folder for generated external help (default: en-US).
Optional file name override for external help (default: <ModuleName>-help.xml).
Path to the folder where documentation will be created.
Path to the readme file that will be used for the documentation.
Disable conversion of about_* topics into markdown pages.
Disable external help (MAML) generation.
Disable generating basic fallback examples for cmdlets missing examples.
When enabled, the generated external help file is also synced back to the project root (e.g. en-US\<ModuleName>-help.xml).

maml:description.maml:para: Creates a module pipeline lifecycle action.

Lifecycle actions run PowerShell at a named module pipeline context point such as AfterStaging, AfterManifest, or BeforePublish. PowerForge writes a stable JSON context file before each action and exposes its path through the POWERFORGE_CONTEXT environment variable.

Use lifecycle actions for project-specific preparation, generated-file adjustments, artifact checks, and release guardrails that need a precise pipeline context. Configuration segment order does not control execution order; the At value does.
Stable module pipeline lifecycle point where the action runs.
When set, logs a failed action and lets the pipeline continue.
When set, disables the action while keeping it in configuration.
Environment variable overrides passed to the action process.
Inline PowerShell script text.
Friendly action name shown in progress and result output.
When set, prefer Windows PowerShell before pwsh on Windows.
Action timeout in seconds. Defaults to five minutes.
Optional working directory. Relative paths resolve from the project root.
Stable module pipeline lifecycle point where the action runs.
When set, logs a failed action and lets the pipeline continue.
When set, disables the action while keeping it in configuration.
Environment variable overrides passed to the action process.
Path to a PowerShell script file. Relative paths resolve from the project root.
Friendly action name shown in progress and result output.
When set, prefer Windows PowerShell before pwsh on Windows.
Action timeout in seconds. Defaults to five minutes.
Optional working directory. Relative paths resolve from the project root.
Stable module pipeline lifecycle point where the action runs.
When set, logs a failed action and lets the pipeline continue.
When set, disables the action while keeping it in configuration.
Environment variable overrides passed to the action process.
Friendly action name shown in progress and result output.

maml:description.maml:para: Adds an external asset bundle that is prepared before module staging.

External asset bundles are for files that should be carried inside a module package but are not authored as normal source files, such as offline installers, tooling archives, generated payloads, or mirrored third-party packages. PowerForge downloads or copies the declared files before staging, computes SHA256 values, and writes a manifest alongside the bundle.
When set, disables the bundle while keeping it in configuration.
Files that make up the external asset bundle.
Optional license expression or label written to the generated manifest.
Optional manifest path. Relative paths resolve from the project root; when omitted, manifest.json is written under OutputPath.
Friendly bundle name written to the generated manifest.
Output directory for the downloaded or copied files. Relative paths resolve from the project root.
When set, existing files are used and missing files fail the build instead of downloading or copying sources.
Optional source URI or project URL written to the generated manifest.
Optional bundle version written to the generated manifest.
When set, disables the bundle while keeping it in configuration.

maml:description.maml:para: Provides a way to configure required, external, embedded, or approved modules used in the project.
Emits module dependency configuration segments. These are later used to patch the module manifest and (optionally) install/package dependencies during a build.

Use RequiredModule for dependencies that should appear in the manifest and can also be bundled into build artefacts when New-ConfigurationArtefact -AddRequiredModules is enabled. Use ExternalModule for dependencies that must exist on the target system but should not be bundled into artefacts. Use EmbeddedModule for dependencies that should be bundled under Internals\Modules and installed/imported later by explicit path with Install-ModuleDependency / Import-ModuleDependency.

RequiredModule entries are written to the manifest RequiredModules. ExternalModule entries are written to PrivateData.PSData.ExternalModuleDependencies. EmbeddedModule entries are copied into the built module internals and are not emitted as manifest dependencies. ApprovedModule entries are used by merge/missing-function workflows and are not emitted as manifest dependencies.
Built-in Microsoft.PowerShell.* modules are ignored during manifest refresh because they are inbox runtime modules, not gallery-resolvable dependencies.

Version and Guid values set to Auto or Latest are resolved from installed modules by default. When New-ConfigurationBuild -ResolveMissingModulesOnline is enabled, repository results can be used without installing the dependency first.
Choose only one versioning style per dependency: a minimum version (-Version or -MinimumVersion) or an exact version (-RequiredVersion). Mixing them for the same module is treated as invalid input.
GUID of the dependency module (or Auto). This is most useful when you want manifest validation to lock onto a specific module identity across repositories.
Minimum version of the dependency module (preferred over -Version). Use this when any newer compatible version is acceptable.
Name of the PowerShell module(s) that your module depends on. Multiple names emit one configuration segment per module using the same dependency settings.
Required version of the dependency module (exact match). Use this when consumers and packaging must resolve the exact same version.

Choose between RequiredModule, ExternalModule, EmbeddedModule, and ApprovedModule. RequiredModule is used for manifest and optional packaging, ExternalModule is install-only, EmbeddedModule is bundled under Internals for explicit install/import by path, and ApprovedModule is merge-only.
Minimum version of the dependency module (or Auto/Latest). This is treated the same as -MinimumVersion and cannot be combined with -RequiredVersion.
Source used when resolving Auto/Latest version values.
GUID of the dependency module (or Auto). This is most useful when you want manifest validation to lock onto a specific module identity across repositories.
Minimum version of the dependency module (preferred over -Version). Use this when any newer compatible version is acceptable.
Name of the PowerShell module(s) that your module depends on. Multiple names emit one configuration segment per module using the same dependency settings.
Required version of the dependency module (exact match). Use this when consumers and packaging must resolve the exact same version.

Choose between RequiredModule, ExternalModule, EmbeddedModule, and ApprovedModule. RequiredModule is used for manifest and optional packaging, ExternalModule is install-only, EmbeddedModule is bundled under Internals for explicit install/import by path, and ApprovedModule is merge-only.
Minimum version of the dependency module (or Auto/Latest). This is treated the same as -MinimumVersion and cannot be combined with -RequiredVersion.
Source used when resolving Auto/Latest version values.

command:example.maml:title: EXAMPLE 1

maml:description.maml:para: Provides a way to configure publishing to PowerShell Gallery, GitHub, JFrog Artifactory, or other private PowerShell module repositories.

This cmdlet emits publish configuration consumed by Invoke-ModuleBuild / Build-Module. Use -Type to choose a destination. For repository publishing, -Tool selects the provider (PowerShellGet/PSResourceGet/ManagedModule/Auto).

For private repositories (for example Azure DevOps Artifacts, JFrog Artifactory, GitHub Packages, or private NuGet v3 feeds), provide repository URIs and (optionally) credentials, or use provider-specific preset parameters to resolve those URIs automatically. To avoid secrets in source control, pass API keys/tokens via -FilePath or environment-specific tooling.

JFrog Artifactory can be configured directly with -JFrogBaseUri and -JFrogRepository. For PAT/basic-auth feeds, use repository credentials only. Add -FilePath or -ApiKey only when the feed requires a separate NuGet API key for package push.
Publish GitHub release as a release even if module prerelease is set.
Enable publishing to the chosen destination.
When true, registers/updates the repository before publishing. Default: true.
API key to be used for publishing in clear text in a file. For JFrog, use this only when the feed requires a separate NuGet API key.
Allow publishing lower version of a module on a PowerShell repository.
When set, asks GitHub to generate release notes automatically.
Optional ID of the artefact used for publishing.
Override tag name used for GitHub publishing.
When set, publishes missing manifest RequiredModules to the target repository before publishing the main module.
Repository API version for PSResourceGet registration (v2/v3).
Repository credential secret (password/token) in clear text. For JFrog PAT/basic-auth flows, this is the PAT or access token.
Environment variable containing the repository credential secret (password/token). For JFrog PAT/access-token flows, this can be JFROG_ACCESS_TOKEN or a CI secret variable.
Repository credential secret (password/token) in a clear-text file. For JFrog PAT/basic-auth flows, prefer this over inline token values.
Repository credential username (basic auth). For JFrog PAT/basic-auth flows, this is the JFrog user name or email.
Repository name override (GitHub or PowerShell repository name).
Repository priority for PSResourceGet (lower is higher priority).
Repository publish URI (PowerShellGet PublishLocation).
Repository source URI (PowerShellGet SourceLocation).
Whether to mark the repository as trusted (avoids prompts). Default: true.
Repository base URI (used for both source and publish unless overridden).
Repository used as the source for publishing missing RequiredModules. Defaults to PSGallery.
Repository URL or local feed path used by managed publishing as the source for missing RequiredModules.
Publishing tool/provider used for repository publishing. Ignored for GitHub publishing.
Choose between PowerShellGallery and GitHub.
When set, unregisters the repository after publish if it was created by this run.
Use this PowerShell repository as the source for resolving Auto/Latest dependency versions.

maml:description.maml:para: Creates repo-level release coordination settings for a module and package build.
Creates repo-level release coordination settings for a module and package build.
Preferred build order for high-level release lanes.
Primary package/project used when the version source is a package/project build. Required by SynchronizeModuleVersion to identify the package coordinated with module history.
Preferred publish order for destinations such as NuGet, PowerShellGallery, and GitHub.
Staged release root where upload-ready assets should be copied.

Coordinates the module and selected primary package on one version. The next available module version becomes a floor for the primary package; a higher numeric package version still wins. At the same numeric version, a stable X-pattern candidate does not erase the configured module prerelease; explicit prerelease versions retain normal semantic-version ordering. PrimaryProject is required, and exactly one selected lane must run before the module and use UseAsReleaseVersionSource. Package groups using AlignPackageVersions are raised together. Publish runs persist a credential-free checkpoint so a partial release resumes the exact versions and skips destinations that already completed.
Explicit release version used when VersionSource is Manual.
Source used to resolve the coordinated release version.
Preferred build order for high-level release lanes.
Primary package/project used when the version source is a package/project build. Required by SynchronizeModuleVersion to identify the package coordinated with module history.
Preferred publish order for destinations such as NuGet, PowerShellGallery, and GitHub.
Staged release root where upload-ready assets should be copied.

Coordinates the module and selected primary package on one version. The next available module version becomes a floor for the primary package; a higher numeric package version still wins. At the same numeric version, a stable X-pattern candidate does not erase the configured module prerelease; explicit prerelease versions retain normal semantic-version ordering. PrimaryProject is required, and exactly one selected lane must run before the module and use UseAsReleaseVersionSource. Package groups using AlignPackageVersions are raised together. Publish runs persist a credential-free checkpoint so a partial release resumes the exact versions and skips destinations that already completed.
Explicit release version used when VersionSource is Manual.
Source used to resolve the coordinated release version.

maml:description.maml:para: Creates configuration for module validation checks during build.

Adds a single validation segment that can run structure, documentation, test, binary, and csproj checks. Each check can be configured as Off/Warning/Error to control whether it is informational or blocking.
Encoding and line-ending enforcement is handled by New-ConfigurationFileConsistency.

Use -SkipDuplicate for CI-friendly, idempotent runs.

maml:description.maml:para: Repairs and verifies installed PowerShell modules through the managed module-state engine.

This command is the managed operator surface for module estate maintenance. It inventories installed modules, plans stale-version, receipt-drift, source, scope, family, and cleanup actions, and can apply the plan through the managed delivery engine.

PSResourceGet has no equivalent estate-repair command. Use the lifecycle cmdlets for one requested operation and this command when the desired outcome spans installed-state discovery, drift analysis, repair, and cleanup.

Repair keeps module copies in separate physical roots, PowerShell editions, scopes, and local user profiles independent. Missing modules require an explicit ModuleRoot or exactly one eligible scanned root; ambiguous destinations are reported and blocked. A single explicit UserProfilePath supplies the current PowerShell edition's standard CurrentUser root even when that root does not exist yet; it never overrides an AllUsers request. Explicit ModuleRoot and profile destinations are merged into supplied Inventory or InventoryPath artifacts and remain part of convergence scans. Module roots declared by maintenance receipts are merged the same way, including roots that do not exist until repair delivery creates them. A successfully enumerated explicit root replaces stale artifact rows and diagnostics for that root, even when the live root is empty.

Live apply performs delivery, inventories the same estate again, replans exact-path old-version cleanup from current state. Cleanup requires that refreshed plan to be error-free, preflights the complete exact-path removal set, validates loaded-module and dependency safety across relevant global/profile roots, and removes selected dependents before their selected dependencies. Current-runspace loaded modules are protected even when IncludeLoaded is not used for inventory output. A declined delivery or cleanup action is reported as skipped and cannot be reported as successful convergence. Repair performs a final live inventory and returns post-apply plan and convergence evidence after execution or a no-action apply. Operational failures remain visible in the typed result and are also written as nonterminating errors.

This is local-machine estate management suitable for workstations and servers, including service-account and multi-profile roots. It does not connect to or orchestrate remote computers; invoke it in each target session or through the operator's existing remoting/configuration system.

It accepts names or typed Find-ManagedModule output, saves unpacked modules or .nupkg files, and defaults to the current filesystem location when Path is omitted.

maml:description.maml:para: Creates a new release for the given GitHub repository and optionally uploads assets.

This cmdlet uses the GitHub REST API to create a release and upload assets. It is a lower-level building block used by higher-level helpers (such as Publish-GitHubReleaseAsset) and can also be used directly in CI pipelines.
Provide the token via an environment variable to avoid leaking secrets into logs or history.
The full paths of the files to include as release assets.
Commitish value that determines where the Git tag is created from.
When set, asks GitHub to generate release notes automatically (cannot be used with ReleaseNotes).
GitHub personal access token used for authentication.
GitHub repository name.
GitHub username owning the repository.
True to create a draft (unpublished) release.
True to identify the release as a prerelease.
The name of the release. If omitted, TagName is used.
The text describing the contents of the release.
When true (default), a 422 tag conflict reuses the existing release. When false, the cmdlet fails on existing tags.
The tag name used for the release.

maml:description.maml:para: Displays module documentation (README, CHANGELOG, LICENSE, Intro/Upgrade) in the console.

Resolves documentation files from an installed module (root or Internals folder) and renders them with Spectre.Console. When local files are absent or when requested, it can fetch files directly from the module's repository specified by PrivateData.PSData.ProjectUri (GitHub or Azure DevOps), optionally using a Personal Access Token.
Displays module documentation (README, CHANGELOG, LICENSE, Intro/Upgrade) in the console.

Resolves documentation files from an installed module (root or Internals folder) and renders them with Spectre.Console. When local files are absent or when requested, it can fetch files directly from the module's repository specified by PrivateData.PSData.ProjectUri (GitHub or Azure DevOps), optionally using a Personal Access Token.
Disable code tokenizers and render code fences as plain text.
Do not open the generated HTML after export.
Controls how examples are rendered: ProseFirst (remarks then code), MamlDefault (code then remarks), or AllAsCode. Default is ProseFirst.
Controls how Examples are sourced. Raw = parse the EXAMPLES section from Get-Help text; Maml = use structured help object; Auto = Raw then Maml.
Convenience switch equal to -SkipRemote -SkipDependencies -SkipCommands.
Show a specific file by name (relative to module root or Internals) or full path.
Heading rulers style. H1AndH2 draws rules for H1/H2, H1 for H1 only, None disables.
Render command help inside fenced code blocks for uniform monospace formatting.
Per-command Get-Help timeout in seconds. Default 3.
Limit the number of commands rendered in the Commands tab. Default 100.
Selection policy for standard tabs when Local and Remote exist (applies only when -Online): PreferLocal (default), PreferRemote, or All.

Open the generated HTML in the default shell handler after export.

maml:description.maml:para: Uninstalls installed PowerShell module versions through the managed module engine.

This command removes modules from the selected managed module root without invoking PowerShellGet or PSResourceGet. It follows PSResourceGet-shaped uninstall selection semantics while adding managed dependency and loaded-module safety checks. InstalledLocation always selects the exact installed directory, whether it is bound directly or received from Get-ManagedModule pipeline output. Typed Get-ManagedModule rows retain structured scanned-root provenance, so dependency checks preserve edition/profile visibility and require roots that were available during inventory to remain available. Multiple piped rows are preflighted as one batch across physical roots and selected dependents are removed before their selected dependencies.
Allow removal of module versions that appear loaded in the current PowerShell session.
Installed module rows returned by Get-ManagedModule to uninstall.
Loaded module evidence used to block risky in-session uninstalls.
Return an inspectable uninstall plan without removing files.
Skip checking whether removed modules are still required by other installed modules.
Allow removal of module versions that appear loaded in the current PowerShell session.
Exact installed location supplied by Get-ManagedModule pipeline rows. It takes precedence over ModuleRoot.
Loaded module evidence used to block risky in-session uninstalls.
Explicit module root. Use with Scope Custom.

Return an inspectable uninstall plan without removing files.

maml:description.maml:para: Updates installed PowerShell modules through the managed C# module engine.

This command provides the module-update functionality of Update-PSResource. It inspects the selected module root and updates only when the repository contains a newer selected version, or installs a named target when the selected scope has no copy. When Name is omitted, all installed modules in the selected roots are considered. Hash-constrained pipeline input is buffered and validated as one package target before any update starts.
Accept package licenses when packages declare license acceptance is required.
Allow command exports to overlap with other modules in the target root.
Allowed package author values from package metadata.
Allow updating even when a matching module is loaded in the current session or supplied as evidence.
Validate Authenticode signatures for signable package files before promotion.
